requires.note: macOS or Linux — the live deck reads running processes via pgrep / ps / lsof, so the dashboard will not populate on Windows.

apps.name: Claude 5IQ
apps.tagline: The five systems that take Claude Code from clever to genuinely sharp — taught in plain language, then switched on without leaving the page.
apps.description:
**Claude 5IQ** is a guided setup for the five systems that take Claude Code from clever to genuinely sharp — each a **+1 IQ** beat, wrapped in a live deck that reports your *real* machine. It teaches every system in plain language, then lets you **turn it on without leaving the page**.

**The five systems**

- **Session Codes** — one session id → a callsign, emoji, and colour, computed by one hash the terminal, dashboard, and browser all agree on.
- **The Status Bar** — the Claude Code statusline dissected segment by segment; wire it into your settings (merge, never clobber — backed up first).
- **The Horse Browser** — a one-WebSocket CDP harness driving a dedicated Chrome that never steals focus, with a live Agent Monitor wall.
- **gwx** — one safe CLI over every Google account; reads fan out in parallel and any multi-account write is refused.
- **CLAUDE.md** — global vs project notes drawn as stacked paper; install the four chapters with a backup.

A sticky live deck reports tools on your PATH, the browser CDP, running sessions as emoji codenames, and known gwx accounts — condensing as you scroll.
